result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: To minimize probing cost, implement a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) framework to optimize the sequence of probes. The state space consists of all possible valid placements of the known polyomino shapes on the grid. The observation model accounts for the noise in aggregate queries (e.g., a query returning a count of oil cells with a probability distribution). Algorithm Steps: 1. State Representation: Maintain a belief distribution b(s) over all possible configurations of oil-containing cells that are consistent with the known polyomino shapes. 2. Action Selection: At each step, choose an action a (either a single-cell drill or an aggregate query) that maximizes the Expected Information Gain (EIG) or minimizes the expected future cost to reach a high-confidence state. - Use aggregate queries early to prune the vast majority of invalid configurations. - Use single-cell drilling only when the belief distribution is highly concentrated on a few specific cells to confirm presence/absence. 3. Update: Upon receiving a noisy aggregate result z, update the belief using Bayes' Rule: b'(s) proportional to P(z|s)b(s). 4. Termination: Stop when the entropy of the belief distribution falls below a threshold where a final guess (the MAP estimate) is likely to be correct with high probability. JUSTIFICATION: Greedy template matching or simple scanning ignores the probabilistic value of aggregate queries. A POMDP approach treats the problem as an information-gathering task, balancing the high cost of drilling against the high information gain (and lower cost) of noisy aggregate queries to prune the search space efficiently.
